programme.title: Les débatteurs de Noovo
programme.sub-title: Campagne électorale : Est-ce que les débats des chefs influencent votre vote?
programme.desc: Nos débatteurs : Me Nada Boumeftah, le journaliste Yves Boisvert, l'analyste Antonine Yaccarni et l'entrepreneur Francois Lambert.

programme.title: Les débatteurs de Noovo
programme.sub-title: Pour ou contre l'augmentation de la limite de vitesse à 120km/h sur les autoroutes?
programme.desc: Nos débatteurs : Me Nada Boumeftah, François Lambert, Geneviève Pettersen, le journaliste automobile Benoit Charette.

programme.title: Les débatteurs de Noovo
programme.sub-title: A-t-on vraiment besoin d'un 3e lien à Québec?
programme.desc: Nos débatteurs : Antonine Yaccarini, Yves Boisvert, Déborah Cherenfant et notre invité l'urbaniste Richard Bergeron.

programme.title: Les débatteurs de Noovo
programme.sub-title: Pour ou contre faire plus de place au privé dans le système de santé?
programme.desc: Nos débatteurs : Geneviève Pettersen, Victor Henriquez, Dr Alain Vadeboncoeur et Me Nada Boumeftah.

programme.title: Les débatteurs de Noovo
programme.sub-title: Pour ou contre, laisser travailler les jeunes de moins de 14 ans?
programme.desc: Nos débatteurs : François Lambert, Déborah Cherenfant, Dominic Vallières et Maude Goyer.

programme.title: Les débatteurs de Noovo
programme.sub-title: L'intérêt pour la culture populaire québécoise est-il en déclin?
programme.desc: Nos débatteurs : Alex Perron, Geneviève Pettersen, Victor Henriquez et Catherine Beauchamp.

programme.title: Les débatteurs de Noovo
programme.sub-title: Trouvez-vous qu'il y a trop de sondages en campagne électorale?
programme.desc: Nos débatteurs : François Lambert, Déborah Cherenfant, Dominic Vallières et notre invité Philippe J. Fournier.

programme.title: Les débatteurs de Noovo
programme.sub-title: Pensez-vous qu'augmenter les seuils d'immigration pourrait être un problème pour le Québec?
programme.desc: Nos débatteurs : Antonine Yaccarini, Victor Henriquez, Nada Boumeftah et François Lambert.

programme.title: Les débatteurs de Noovo
programme.sub-title: Pour ou contre que les 5 chefs des partis obtiennent automatiquement un siège à l'Assemblée nationale?
programme.desc: Nos débatteurs : Geneviève Pettersen, Dominic Vallières, Victor Henriquez et l'invité Daniel Turp.

programme.title: Les débatteurs de Noovo
programme.sub-title: À votre avis, le Québec est-il plus divisé en ce lendemain d'élection? OUI ou NON
programme.desc: Nos Débatteurs : Geneviève Pettersen, Victor Henriquez, Dominic Vallières et Toula Drimonis.

programme.title: Les débatteurs de Noovo
programme.sub-title: Les athlètes qui ont commis des gestes de nature criminelle devraient-ils être bannis de leur sport? OUI ou NON

programme.title: Blue Ridge
programme.desc: Ex-Green Beret, Justin Wise, trades in his life of danger for a sheriff badge in a sleepy town at the heart of the Blue Ridge Mountains. Now closer to his ex-wife and daughter, he's ready to settle down and embrace a life without chaos. But trouble has a way of following him. Just when he starts getting adjusted to the simple life, a murder in the community puts the residents on edge and refuels a longtime feud between two of the most powerful families. As Justin and his deputies work the case, he realizes there is more to his new home than meets the eye. Secrets unfold, and motives emerge, leading Justin on a race against time to solve the case before the townspeople take the law into their own hands.

programme.title: AFTCO Original Film
programme.desc: Set in Wisconsin, “Up North” explores the passion and pursuit of the midwestern angler. Whether they're breaking ice on Green Bay to chase trophy walleye or traveling up to the Northwoods to carry on years of family traditions, Kaitlin and Josh Woodward embody a passion for fishing in its truest form. After fishing together for nearly 11 years, the Woodwards started a part-time guiding business in 2019 called Woodward Fishing to share their passion for their own Wisconsin waters. Some of Kaitlin's favorite memories on the water have been in Green Bay chasing trophy fish she recounts, “Every cast was a pre-spawn 25” -27” walleye it was unreal.” Their teamwork and drive on the water are unmatched. Josh brings his determination to find the fish, and Kaitlin brings her tenacity and ability to get everyone back to the purity of nature no matter what the day brings. They have guided together for the past four years in the Northwoods of Wisconsin and Green Bay, and both areas hold an equally special place for the Woodwards. Josh cut his teeth fishing in Green Bay and Sturgeon Bay with his grandfather and grew up going to the Northwoods with his family. These traditions have become a way of life for Josh and Kaitlin as their year revolves around making the most of the season in both locations with their favorite boat dog, Kimber, in tow. “My family was here before my time, and now we're going to pass that on to our kids.” – Josh Woodward Follow their adventures as they put AFTCO fishing rain gear to the test in our newest AFTCO Original Film, “Up North.”

programme.title: Winchester Life
programme.desc: On opening day of Missouri's gun season, hunters from around the state seek ridges and box blinds throughout the “Show Me” state in search of mature whitetail bucks. For Nikki Boxler and the Winchester Deer Camp, it's no different. With Winchester's in hand, Nikki and crew play cat and mouse with several mature bucks at Indian Hill Outfitters in Pike County.

programme.title: Winchester Life
programme.desc: Every season, just like clockwork, mission-driven longboards gobble their way across the plains of South Dakota, the cornfields of Iowa, the timbered ridges of Michigan and the cattle ground of Kansas looking for their soulmates in one of the most celebrated hunting seasons in America…the wild turkey breeding season. Join Team Winchester as they stalk, call, flank and roost boss gobblers into range from coast to coast.

programme.title: Baby Einstein
programme.sub-title: Baby Da Vinci: From Head to Toe
programme.desc: From the day their eyes open, babies begin to observe new things around the world and about themselves. Baby Da Vinci From Head to Toe presents them with a playful and interactive introduction to the human body in three languages -- English, Spanish, and French. With a whimsical blend of adorable puppets, real children and animals and famous works of art, this captivating program also features upbeat classical music.

programme.title: Baby Einstein
programme.desc: World Music introduces you and your little one to beautiful sounds from every continent, bringing a fresh, new experience of music and culture. Sing, dance and play to exotic rhythms that open the door to an appreciation of different cultures from around the world.

programme.title: Baby Einstein
programme.sub-title: Baby's First Sounds: Discoveries for Little Ears
programme.desc: Perhaps the sweetest sounds you'll ever hear are the babbles and coos that one day become ma-ma, da-da and I love you. Besides being music to your ears, these baby steps of language change everything as your little one discovers an exciting, powerful new way of relating to the world! Baby's First Sounds invites both of you to share these memorable moments: those precious ''firsts'' that come around once in your baby's lifetime.

programme.title: Baby Einstein
programme.desc: From the moment babies are born, they use their developing senses to discover the world. In terms of sight, bold patterns in contrasting colors or black and white are initially preferred. As for sounds, Mother s gentle voice, in any language, is music to baby's ears. Language Nursery: Voices From Many Lands acquaints your baby with the sounds of foreign language by presenting delightful, visually stimulating images accompanied by spoken passages, natural sounds and music.

programme.title: Baby Einstein
programme.desc: Between 12 and 24 months, little ones become increasingly interested in more complex ideas and begin to explore numbers and counting. Numbers Nursery introduces little ones to numerals 1 through 5 in a captivating, interactive way that invites them to join in, clap their hands and count out loud.

programme.title: Baby Einstein
programme.desc: As babies grow and change, so does the landscape around them. From fiery red leaves to falling snowflakes from flowers in bloom to fireworks in July, every season of the year has its own special magic just waiting to be discovered.

programme.title: Baby Einstein
programme.desc: Your little one will want to keep a beat, snap his fingers and march his feet! Join new characters Pillie, the musical woodpecker, and Coco, the dancing cockatoo, and share the excitement of rhythm with your baby. World of Rhythm explores rhythm in music, rhythm in nature and rhythm as a form of expression.

programme.title: Baby Einstein
programme.desc: At 12 months, babies can identify specific sounds, and they re also starting to match the sounds they hear with the objects that make them. Meet the Orchestra -- First Instruments presents your little "maestro" with a playful introduction to the sights and sounds of a real orchestra -- from the clashing of cymbals to the purring of the cello and everything in between!

programme.title: Baby Einstein
programme.desc: As each day passes, babies' awareness of the world around them -- and all the wonders it holds -- continues to develop. Wild animals are especially intriguing and World Animals captures the spirtit of little ones' fascination with wild creatures as it introduces them to animals living in the jungle, in the ocean, and on the savannah.

programme.title: Baby Einstein
programme.desc: Take your little explorer on an exotic musical expedition to meet wild animals from across the globe. Journey to the jungle, savannah, desert and forest.

programme.title: Baby Einstein
programme.desc: Learn about the numbers 1,2,3,4 & 5 through rhyme, real-life footage, counting, stories, and repetition - accompanied by classical music and real world imagery.

programme.title: Bob the Builder

programme.desc: Farmer Pickles needs Bob to build a hencoop. / Wendy is holding a surprise party for her sister Jenny.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Bob is building some traffic calming measures. Bob has been working all night laying lino.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.sub-title: Spud The Dragon / Pilchard Steals The Show
programme.desc: Bob is insulating Mrs. Potts' loft. Wendy is building an enclosure for the local dog show.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Bob's new metal detector is delivered. / Bob is installing a new pizza oven for Mr Sabatini.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.sub-title: Wendy's Removal Service / Mr Beasley's DIY Disaster
programme.desc: Bob is carrying out a loft conversion at a new home. / Bob is building a lean-to for Mr Beasley's allotment.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.sub-title: Bob's Egg And Spoon Race / Trix's Tiles
programme.desc: The whole town is practicing for an egg and spoon race. / Bob is tiling a roof.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.sub-title: Spud's Big Splash / Spud The Musketeer
programme.desc: Bob is building a water feature in Mrs Potts' garden. / Wendy is making a floral display for the town square.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.sub-title: Wendy's Magic Birthday / Bob's Egg And Spoon Race
programme.desc: It is Wendy's birthday, but she doesn't want any fuss. / The town is practicing for an egg and spoon race.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.sub-title: Mr Beasley's New Friends / Spud The Pilot
programme.desc: Bob is repairing a leaking water tank at school. / Bob is installing an antique fireplace for Mrs Percival.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.sub-title: Mr Ellis's Exhibition / Bob and the Badgers
programme.desc: Bob is demolishing an old house. Farmer Pickles needs a fence built to keep the rabbits out.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.sub-title: Bob and the Goalie / Dizzy Goes Camping
programme.desc: Bob is building a new exercise park. Farmer Pickles has decided to open a camp site on his land.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: The Mayor asks Bob to build a Pets Corner in a local park. / A burst pipe outside Mr Sabatini's needs fixing.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.sub-title: Dizzy the Sheepdog / Dizzy Goes Camping
programme.desc: Bob and team are building a barn for Farmer Pickles. / Farmer Pickles wants to open a camp site on his land.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.sub-title: Bob The Photographer / Mr Bentley's Trains
programme.desc: Bob goes to Farmer Pickles farm to take some photographs. / Mrs Bentley asks Bob to build a new model railway.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.sub-title: Where, Oh, Where Has Our Blueberry Gone?
programme.desc: Blueberry has become obsessed with the mystery book detective heroine, Patti Persimmon. She decorates her store with Patti posters and rearranges her shelves to make room for all the Patti books she's ordered. Blueberry begins to neglect outings with her friends and even goes so far as to dress like her idol just to emulate her. She then starts looking for cases to solve around town.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: Strawberry Shortcake's friends watch her with wide eyes as she everrrrr-so-gently squirts a layer of blueberry smoothie into a tall glass, trying to not disturb the five other multi-colored layers beneath it. It's her tenth attempt to create the world's first Rainbow Smoothie! She has even painted one wall of her café rainbow colors in honor of the soon-to-be new item on the menu. No one dares breathe for fear of spoiling the delicate touch of a master smoothie-squirter at work.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: After returning to the café after a walk with her pets, Strawberry is met by Mr. Longface. He's been waiting for her so that they can get started making treats and decorating for Sadiebug's and Kadiebug's party that afternoon. But Custard and Pupcake have enjoyed their time outside so much that they want to continue playing.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: While sipping cider in Strawberry's café, the girls muse about how they cannot wait for winter to arrive. They list the various traditions they love about the season but the one they all agree on is the Glimmerberry Ball. Strawberry mentions that she doesn't think the café can hold the party this year because of how the town has grown. The girls decide to split up and search for a new location.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: From the BerryWorks to Strawberry's Café, everyone is excited about who will win this year's Spring Flower Festival! Berry Bitty City is decorated with colorful banners proclaiming the big event. Lemon works the hardest. For weeks she has watered her violets by day and sung to them at night, doing everything she can to win first prize. With the contest just a day away, Lemon is at her garden early.

programme.title: Berry Bitty Grand Opening
programme.desc: Strawberry is about to open her new café. She sends the Berrykins to pick the items she needs from her friends. Unfortunately, the Berrykins are so excited that they mishear or forget what Strawberry told them.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: Berrykin Bloom's braggart cousin, Berrykin Bertram, has won the hot sauce competition at the local county fair year after year.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.sub-title: When the Berry Fairy Came to Stay
programme.desc: Out gathering berries one day, Strawberry & her pets find a tiny fairy among the berry vines. Realizing that Strawberry is a very kind & caring person, the little fairy decides to take advantage of the kid's good nature.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: Honey Pie Pony is excited that the ponies of Ice Cream Island have invited her to take part in their annual Festival of the Horses.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: Strawberry Shortcake's friendship letter to children around the world elicits an invitation from Crepes Suzette to visit Pearis and the Pieffel Tower. Some of the other children and Custard are not so sure that it is wise to visit a strange land.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: This is the story of "Rapunzel", but with a twist. Princess Rapunzel (Rainbow Sherbet) has always preferred being called Rap and liked doing things her way.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: When Strawberry & her friends decide to put on a talent show, they plan to enter themselves as a band, The Strawberry Jams!

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: Peppermint Fizz is so determined to win the Nearly Once-A-Yearly Strawberryland Games, she resorts to cheating.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: At the urging of Honey Pie Pony, Strawberry & Friends decide to put on a Cooking Show. Ginger Snap invents a version of TV, which she calls Berryvision, to broadcast the show all over Strawberryland.

programme.title: HappyKids
programme.desc: HappyKids

programme.title: HappyKids
programme.desc: HappyKids

programme.title: HappyKids
programme.desc: HappyKids

programme.title: HappyKids
programme.desc: HappyKids

programme.title: HappyKids
programme.desc: HappyKids

programme.title: A Brush with Teeth
programme.desc: At school, Sid discovers that our mouths are filled with different kinds of teeth that do lots of different jobs--like chewing our food! So it's really important to keep our teeth healthy by brushing them every day!

programme.title: Sky's the Limit
programme.desc: When a storm causes a giant boulder to cut off Berry Bitty City's water supply, Strawberry and the girls face the possibility of having to move away from their homes. With the help of Mr. Longface Caterpillar, the girls set out on an adventure to save their town.

programme.title: Tarzan and Jane
programme.desc: After returning to London, Tarzan and Jane encounter Jane's mother, Angela, a celebrity-cum-journalist who is researching an expose claiming Greystoke Industries harms the environment. The truth is someone is trying to destroy Greystoke Industries. Tarzan and Jane must figure out who this mystery saboteur is while battling fires, snakes, and sneak attacks. With this Mastermind's henchmen trying to stop them at every turn, our young heroes must take part in a thrilling, multi-country adventure in order to save Greystoke Industries and finally put an end to the criminal enterprise that threatens to put the environment and countless lives - human and animal alike - in danger!

programme.title: Blippi's Great Green Adventure
programme.desc: In this special episode, Blippi celebrates Earth Day with his Great Green Adventure. Caring for the environment is one of our prime responsibilities and Blippi teaches children how by taking them on a puzzle-solving adventure.

programme.title: Starlight, Star Bright
programme.desc: With time running out before the Grand Opening celebration for the new Marketplace, Strawberry's friends get the idea to invite her favorite singer, superstar CHERRY JAM, to be the guest singer. The girls are blown away when their berryjuice cell phone gets a text message that Cherry accepts their invitation! Strawberry is touched by their surprise, but reminds them that now the town and Marketplace really have to shine! The girls and Berrykins rush to turn the Marketplace Café into a suitable performance area for a big star like Cherry Jam.

programme.title: HappyKids
programme.desc: HappyKids

programme.title: Link into the VRAINS
programme.desc: n Den City, duels are held in the highly advanced VR world Link VRAINS, which is always under attack by the Knights of Hanoi. However, a hero named Playmaker has arrived to face the Knights, capturing a program they want called an Ignis.

programme.title: Seize the Wind!
programme.desc: Playmaker continues his duel with the Knight of Hanoi using the new Speed Duel rules. Playmaker is backed into a corner by the Knight's Cracking Dragon, but a data storm soon envelopes the field and Playmaker activates his Skill.

programme.title: Jelly Jamm
programme.desc: Jelly Jamm celebrates music, fun and friendship.

programme.title: Splash And Bubbles
programme.sub-title: Seal Sitters/ From Ray To Zee
programme.desc: Splash and Bubbles stop to visit Tidy / Splash and Bubbles learn that Zee has a distant relative in Reeftown.

programme.title: Splash And Bubbles
programme.sub-title: The Sea Sparkles/ Tyke And Seek
programme.desc: Splash, Bubbles, Dunk, and Ripple play a big game of hide and seek with Tyke.

programme.title: Leo, The Wildlife Ranger
programme.desc: Can the Junior Rangers get a baby Komodo Dragon to a safe place before the menacing adults get to it?

programme.title: Leo, The Wildlife Ranger
programme.desc: Can the Junior Rangers train a timid Caracal to be independent in the wild?

programme.title: Counting With Paula
programme.desc: The children lose their board game dice. They go the Desert in search of new dice.

programme.title: Counting With Paula
programme.sub-title: Snakes And A Plane / A Gift Fit For A Prince
programme.desc: Paula and her friends meet a prince and his overbearing guard in the Desert.

programme.title: Counting With Paula
programme.desc: Mr. Banana Tree is feeling lonely. He's always stuck in the ground and unable to go places.

programme.title: Counting With Paula
programme.sub-title: Billy Takes Centre Stage / Curse of the Ghost Monkey
programme.desc: Something happens and Billy has to take over as the star performer of the Far East Festival.

programme.title: ZooMoo
programme.desc: This episode is all about the wonderful fantastic things that animals can do!

programme.title: ZooMoo
programme.sub-title: Slow Down You Move Too Fast / Green Green Grass
programme.desc: In this episode we look at animals that move really slowwwwww.

programme.title: Music & Sing-Alongs!
programme.desc: Sing along with Billy Bunny as he learns lots of new songs before dinnertime!

programme.title: Counting With Paula
programme.sub-title: Mama Ling's Food Delivery Service / Paula, the Fashion Hat Designer
programme.desc: Mama Ling starts a new food delivery service, but it doesn't go well and her rating drops.

programme.title: The Haunted and the Hunted!
programme.desc: Something mysterious lurking the jungle wrecks a construction site, and Hudson is convinced a ghost is responsible - but it might be something even more surprising.

programme.title: The Snowball Effect
programme.desc: Laval and Li'Ella's relationship grows as he gives her a tour of the Lion City. But distracted, he misses a key battle against the Hunters. Tormak joins the fight and saves the day. But the Lion King Lagravis is captured and brought to the Ice Fort. Who will lead the Lions now?!?

programme.title: Friends Reunite
programme.desc: Olly about how his obsession is affecting their relationships. Olly refuses to see the damage he's causing, but it becomes clear that he's lost touch with what really matters.

programme.title: All Of Nothing
programme.desc: The ninja and Sensei Wu try to infiltrate the Serpentines' inner sanctum and walk right into a trap, and when Lloyd's rescue mission fails, there is only one person who can help them, namely the evil Lord Garmadon – but can they trust him?

programme.title: LEGO Marvel Super Heroes: Maximum Overload
programme.desc: Spider-Man and Marvel's Super Heroes take on a mischievous Loki and a team of super villains in an all-new LEGO adventure

programme.title: Back To School
programme.desc: When the NEXO-Knights are sloppy in a fight with Jestro, Merlok 2.0 sends them back to the Knights' Academy. Not only for a brush-up but to find out what Jestro is after in its hallowed halls. As they Knights battle Jestro – in a somewhat haphazard and lazy way – they discuss the fact that Jestro never wins and that they're always getting over on him. Despite defeating the jerky Jester and his evil Book, Merlok 2.0 discovers that something about the attack on the village is tied to the Knights' Academy. It looks like that's where they'll strike next. It wouldn't hurt for the Knights to go “back to school” to watch for Jestro and for some refresher training. Merlok is sure Jestro wants something at the Academy, but he doesn't know what it is. The Knights are very much “fish out of water” at the academy, even with the “cool kids” – Robin and Ava – showing them around. The next generation of Knights is being taught to integrate the NEXO-Magic of Merlok into their fighting techniques and these kids are good (so good, they initially kick the lazy, know-it-all Knight's butts!) Their old principal, the ever-ornery Headmaster Brickland, sets them straight: they're all still slackers, and they better get their acts together (especially Lance!) The Knights take shifts as “library helpers” much to the delight of the old squirebot librarian, MARGE. They're convinced Jestro is after a book…isn't he always after a book? When Jestro attacks, the Knights help organize the students and there's a big fight in the Academy Library versus Jestro and his Monsters. They drive Jestro off without letting him steal any books! Victory! But as Jestro leaves, we see him holding what the Book of Monsters really wanted: a snow globe with a castle in it that was sitting on the librarian's desk. In the Lava Lands, Jestro throws the snow globe into a volcano and it frees the massive Castle of Monstrux from its snowy magic prison

programme.title: The CHI Jackers
programme.desc: The Wolves have become strong because of the stolen CHI. And now they are stealing even more. Soon all the other tribes of CHIMA have none. Laval and Eris must find a new, clever and “Wolf proof ” way to get CHI to the tribes.

programme.title: Symptoms!
programme.desc: Owen falls prey to the toxin of a poisonous prehistoric jungle flower and believes he's a raptor.

programme.title: Snow Way
programme.desc: Practicing for the local snow lodge's big snowboard race, Mia has an accident and hurts her ankle. She insists it's no biggie, but when she tries to resume practice, the normally fearless Mia finds she's now terrified of snowboarding! At the local snow lodge's Snowboard Meet, ace snowboarder Mia, trying to raise money for a new Heartlake City animal shelter, enters the fiercest race of all – the Boardercross. Then, while practicing, she has an accident and hurts her ankle. She insists it's no big deal, but once she heals and tries to resume practice, the usually fearless Mia finds she's now terrified of snowboarding! With the help of her Friends, she tries hard to overcome her fear, but her nightmarish visions of snowboard monsters and jeering snowmen have her shaking in her bed. She backs out of the race, disappointing her Friends and herself. Finally, when an avalanche traps Fiona, Mia is forced to overcome her fears and race to the rescue.

programme.title: The Shape of Motion
programme.desc: Arin's quest to find his parents takes an unexpected turn when he and the Ninja stumble upon a baby dragon in need.

programme.title: Enter the City of Temples
programme.desc: Breaking into the heavily guarded City of Temples, they must prove their worth to secure a spot in the tournament.

programme.title: Legacy, Vehicle and Mechs
programme.desc: Zane takes help from Nya for backup memories of their adventures to the Samurai X Cave computer, but Zane encounters a mysterious puzzle buried deep within his code. Nya digitizes Jay and uploads him into Zane's mind. Jay reviews memories of the Ninja's various motorcycles, mechs and planes in order to solve the vehicle-themed puzzle.

programme.title: Spit Take!
programme.desc: A Dilophosaurus runs loose in the park's hotel, and Owen and Claire must find it before the guests notice - and before Vic's dino-hunting security team can damage the hotel and the guests.

programme.title: Grim Vengeance
programme.desc: The Never Witch's ambitions have darkened, leading her to target the Grim Realm. In an unlikely alliance, the dream chasers must join forces with the Nightmare King and his grimspawn minions.

programme.title: Crown Control
programme.desc: The dream chasers stumble upon the fabled Crown of Control with surprising ease, thanks to an unexpected encounter with the King of the Creatures.

programme.title: The Promised Crown Affair
programme.desc: Izzie has vanished into the dream world, and the dream chasers struggle to maintain normalcy in the waking world while secretly searching for her.

programme.title: Friends Reunite
programme.desc: Olly about how his obsession is affecting their relationships. Olly refuses to see the damage he's causing, but it becomes clear that he's lost touch with what really matters.

programme.title: All Of Nothing
programme.desc: The ninja and Sensei Wu try to infiltrate the Serpentines' inner sanctum and walk right into a trap, and when Lloyd's rescue mission fails, there is only one person who can help them, namely the evil Lord Garmadon – but can they trust him?

programme.title: Pokémon
programme.desc: As our heroes and Team Rocket beat the odds by surviving the sinking of the St. Anne, they are dragged into another perilous situation when they are attacked by the Pokémon, Gyarados!

programme.title: Pokémon
programme.desc: When a group of Tentacool interferes with the construction of a hotel, a woman offers a reward to anyone who can repel them. However, our heroes are forced into action when Team Rocket's ploy fails spectacularly!

programme.title: Pokémon
programme.desc: Our heroes arrive at a small port town, in the middle of celebrating a summer festival. To recover from their hard journey, Ash and his friends decide to relax and enjoy the festivities.

programme.title: Pokémon
programme.desc: When the party sees a group of Butterfree flying above the ocean, Ash decides to release his own Butterfree. After being rejected once, can Ash's Butterfree win over the heart of its pink sweetheart?

programme.title: Pokémon
programme.desc: The party finally arrives in Saffron City, where a young girl commands a Pokémon Gym with her psychic powers. If Ash defeats Gym Leader Sabrina, he will earn his fourth Gym badge!

programme.title: Pokémon
programme.desc: After being defeated by Sabrina, Ash realizes that he must catch a Ghost-type Pokémon to defeat the Saffron Gym Leader's awesome Psychic-type Pokémon. He heads for Pokémon Tower in Lavender Town, where they are said to dwell.

programme.title: Pokémon
programme.desc: Leaving Pokémon Tower with Haunter in tow, Ash returns to Saffron City with only one goal in mind: to win a Gym Badge from the powerful Psychic-type Gym Leader, Sabrina!

programme.title: Pokémon
programme.desc: When Ash tries to catch a wild Mankey, it loses its cool and uses Thrash on the whole gang—even stealing Ash's hat! Will Ash find a way to pacify this pugnacious Pokémon?

programme.title: Pokémon
programme.desc: After insulting Gym Leader Erika's perfume at a department store, Ash must go to some outrageous lengths to even set foot in the Celadon Gym, let alone be deemed worthy to challenge her!

programme.title: Pokémon
programme.desc: Officer Jenny reveals that a mysterious sleep wave has been detected in the area for several days. Our heroes suspect this has something to do with why so many children have gone missing, and why the Pokémon at the Pokémon Center arent getting better

programme.title: Pokémon
programme.desc: At Brock's persistent request, our heroes stop by a well-known place to Pokémon breeders for its Pokémon beauty salons.

programme.title: Pokémon
programme.desc: Outside Fuchsia City, our heroes come across Hitmonchan, a Fighting-type Pokémon. With little thought, Ash attempts to battle it, but he and Pikachu are quickly defeated. But who is the mysterious Trainer of this extraordinary Hitmonchan?

programme.title: Pokémon

programme.title: Schitt's Creek
programme.desc: Johnny and Stevie visit a nearby golf course to pitch a package deal with the motel, but Johnny becomes distracted by his desire to play a round of golf. After agreeing to attend Twyla's murder mystery party, Moira must help her recruit other guests. Alexis entertains a suggestion from Ted to go back to school.

programme.title: Schitt's Creek
programme.desc: When David contemplates starting a new business in town, Moira uses her seat on Town Council to protect her son's interests. Alexis discovers high school isn't as easy as it used to be. Johnny reluctantly offers romantic advice to the hapless baker Ivan, in return for help with the motel's continental breakfast.

programme.title: Schitt's Creek
programme.desc: David gets flustered when Ray's assistant Patrick tries to help him set up his business. Johnny is called before Alexis' teacher, but has trouble mounting a defence against the academic charges. Moira's hospitality skills are put to the test when she's forced to deal with a high-maintenance motel guest.

programme.title: Schitt's Creek
programme.desc: Roland and Moira attend a conference of regional town councillors in Elmdale, and score a major coup for Schitt's Creek. However, the ensuing celebration leads to an awkward encounter the following morning. Meanwhile, Johnny and Jocelyn become concerned that their spouses are keeping secrets from them. As David and Patrick prepare the store for opening, David becomes jealous of Alexis's flirting with Patrick.

programme.title: Schitt's Creek
programme.desc: David's photographer ex-boyfriend Sebastien arrives in town to do a photo series on Moira, but she soon realizes his intentions might be less than complimentary. Meanwhile, David confronts the failure of his and Sebastien's relationship. Johnny and Roland attend Bob's poker night, and attempt to turn the tables on Bob and Gwen after realizing they might be conspiring to cheat. Alexis accompanies Ted to a seniors' dance, and begins to reevaluate her relationship with him.

programme.title: Schitt's Creek
programme.desc: Moira is wary after Town Council decides to name a questionable flower garden after her – that is until she learns the real reasoning behind the dubious honour. Meanwhile, Johnny must do damage control after Alexis spreads head lice throughout the motel. In an effort to flee the lice epidemic, David schemes his way into spending the night at Stevie's apartment.

programme.title: Schitt's Creek
programme.desc: Moira tracks down the Roses' gigantic family portrait as a gift for Johnny, but the family struggles to find a place to put it. David organizes a “soft launch” of his new store, but becomes concerned after word begins to spread, and the guest list gets out of hand. Alexis and Ted become awkward around each other after sharing a kiss.

programme.title: Schitt's Creek
programme.desc: Johnny and Stevie are delighted to have an excuse to put up the motel's “No Vacancy” sign for the first time, while Moira and the Jazzagals prepare for an upcoming festival gig, Alexis gets ready for her high school graduation and makes a big decision for her future. David is secretly bitter that the family forgot his birthday, but gets closer to Patrick after the two go on an accidental date.

programme.title: Schitt's Creek
programme.desc: Moira believes she is the cause of a man's death at the motel and fears the legal repercussions. Johnny and Stevie attempt to keep the motel guests distracted while they deal with the removal of the dead body. Alexis helps Ted interview candidates for the vet clinic and uses some unconventional interview techniques. At the store Patrick and David test the waters of their new relationship.

programme.title: Schitt's Creek
programme.desc: After Johnny finds a positive pregnancy test in Alexis's garbage, Johnny and Moira consider the potential implications of an unexpected pregnancy, but when Johnny tries to broach the subject with Alexis, she is distracted with her college enrollment application. David and Patrick desperately search for a place to hook up, and are forced to consider Stevie's offer of a private space.

programme.title: Schitt's Creek
programme.desc: Moira prepares for her one-woman show “One Crazy Summer: the Patty Hearst Story” at the annual Schitt's Creek Asbestos Fest, but with its intricate and complex dance routine, Moira's worries she won't be able to pull it off. Meanwhile, David basks in the constant flattery from a group of teens that hang around the store, but is livid when Alexis points out it might be a big charade. Johnny looks to hire new help for the motel and Roland claims to have the perfect man for the job.

programme.title: Schitt's Creek
programme.desc: Moira chauffeurs Alexis and Twyla on their girls' night out at a bar, doling out unsolicited romantic advice. At the motel Roland builds a man cave in the office instead of doing actual work, forcing Johnny to reconsider Roland's employment. With Stevie's help, Patrick rearranges some products in the store to illustrate David's inability to compromise, and David tries his best to conciliate.

programme.title: Schitt's Creek
programme.desc: Moira is horrified to learn there's a rumour of her death circulating online, but when she reads the heartfelt condolences from her former Hollywood co-stars, she starts to relish it. Meanwhile, Roland asks Johnny to be the godfather to Roland and Jocelyn's child, leading Johnny to search for ways to politely decline. David and Alexis go on a buying trip to a local farm, and Alexis soon realizes that she has a connection to the farmer, which puts her in an awkward position.

programme.title: Schitt's Creek
programme.desc: David and Patrick throw an open mic night to help promote Rose Apothecary, but when Patrick announces he'll be performing, David gets cold feet. Johnny and Alexis begin re-branding the motel, but Stevie is apprehensive, fearing that they're expanding too quickly. Moira accidentally finds out the gender of the Schitts' baby, and tries desperately to keep it a secret.

programme.title: Schitt's Creek
programme.desc: Johnny and Moira throw a family barbecue with the intention of getting to know Patrick a little bit better, as they believe this to be the longest relationship David has ever had. But David is worried about inflicting his family upon Patrick and tries to keep them apart. Meanwhile, Alexis receives a mysterious text from Ted, and with the help of a new friend, decides to investigate.

programme.title: ALF
programme.desc: Willie and Kate miss the days when they would visit friends' dinner parties and then reciprocate. They decide to host a luau with a roasted pig. They invite only the people who've invited them to dinner, but end up with everyone from the office at work. Alf tries to strong arm trash rights, but ends up having to agree to watch from the upstairs. The party is rained out, all the guests have to come inside, a drunk guest sees the family dog making a sandwich. Alf is locked in the shed and breaks out. At the end of the episode, Alf is reading a Transformers comic.

programme.title: ALF

programme.title: Lamuweri Guluka
programme.desc: Lamuweri Guluka is an African name meaning “hard to pronounce on the first try.” Lam is a seasoned comedian who has performed at colleges, comedy clubs, and cafes all over the country whose background as a nerdy, suburban, black male gives him a unique comedic perspective that audiences love.

programme.title: Leighann Lord: I Mean Business
programme.desc: Leighann Lord is an American comedian, writer, and actress. She performs stand-up comedy, has appeared on Tough Crowd with Colin Quinn, and is a former co-host (with Neil deGrasse Tyson) of the Star Talk Radio podcast As of April 2020, Lord became a co-host for Center for Inquiry's Point of Inquiry podcast

programme.title: Rik Roberts
programme.desc: Rik Roberts brings a little bit of Nashville everywhere he goes. From quick bits about family and technology, to surprising stories about marriage and music – you'll find something you can relate to. Growing up in Kentucky, he got his hands dirty working

programme.title: Nazareth
programme.desc: Award Winning Comedian Nazareth, has been seen by over 40 Million people around the world through live concerts, TV and Radio broadcasts. His list of TV appearances include: Comedy Central, ABC Family, NBC, CBS, and TBN.

programme.title: Reno Collier
programme.desc: Reno's journey began at the Punchline Comedy Club in Atlanta, GA where he tended bar to supplement his day job. He found himself on stage during the weekly open mic night. His unique outlook on life - mixed with a six-pack, was instantly popular.

programme.title: Matt Bergman
programme.desc: Matt Bergman talks about being bad at handhsakes, accidentally breaking into an elderly woman's house, and bombing as an opener for the Goo Goo Dolls with sharp and self-deprecating humor.

programme.title: Steve Hofstetter: A Trip to Japan
programme.desc: Often called the "hardest working man in show-business", Steve Hofstetter covers everything from paper decapitations in plane crashes to seeing-eye children.

programme.title: Chad Thornsberry
programme.desc: Chad Thornsberry was born in St. Louis, MO. From an early age he learned his family was blessed, and blessed him with humor, quick wit, and a love of storytelling. Chad's unique comedic perspective awards audience the opportunity to laugh at the common occurrences in everyday life. Often referred to as a great writer, his straightforward style allows audiences to laugh with him and at him as they find the humor in themselves. With reflections on growing up in a trailer park and observations as an English teacher, Chad's satiric insights look at the pitfalls that make us human. With smart humor and a conversational style, Chad has delighted audiences from the trailer park, throughout the Midwest, and from coast to coast.

programme.title: Chris Schlichting: Catch and Dress a Squirrel
programme.desc: Chris Schlichting, an Iowan with every bone in his body still intact, comes with a few crucial warnings. Beware of birthday wishes, day raccoons, and the spelling on all hand written love letters.

programme.title: Jesse Egan
programme.desc: San Diego native Jesse Egan jokes about hitting on women in a smart car, hippie drive-bys, and the gluten free craze.

programme.title: Andy Beningo
programme.desc: Over the past decade, Andy Beningo has established himself as one of the hardest working comedians in the business. Performing his clever, "every man" material has led to some 2,700 career performances. Along the way, he has appeared on AXS Gotham Comedy Live, as a final four finalist on CMT's Next Big Comic, and on the Bob and Tom radio show. Beningo's new CD regularly plays on Laugh USA on XM Sirius Radio. He's been hailed for his clean comedy, and was named one of the 40 Best Up and Coming Comedians in the country. He was also named "Best Comedian" by both the Detroit Metro Times and Detroit Hour magazine.

programme.title: James Johann
programme.desc: James' childhood upbringing was significantly influenced by country music, countless bad hair cuts, and Catholic school. In 1991, James began his stand-up career in Kansas City. Today, James is a long-time veteran of the stand-up comedy circuit, performing at clubs across the country. His self-deprecating sense of humor and high energy style come together to create a unique onstage persona. James relies more on his brain than the profane and his comedy is a truthful and hilarious reflection of life as he sees it.

programme.title: Helen Hong: Well Hong
programme.desc: Comedian/actor Helen Hong spent Covid quarantine trying to date doomsday preppers.

programme.title: Josh Gondelman: People Pleaser

programme.desc: Declassified FBI and CIA documents help unravel the puzzle of Marilyn Monroe's demise, which was ruled a "probable suicide," while providing evidence suggesting that Marilyn was murdered.

programme.title: Unsolved Mysteries: The Sleepwalker Killing
programme.desc: A man admits to murder, but his lawyers try to prove that he wasn't awake when it happened. With Hilary Swank.

programme.title: On the Edge
programme.desc: When a young basketball player becomes entangled with drug dealers, he turns to an ex-cop and former private eye. Starring Ice-T, Gary Busey, Fred Williamson and Jim Brown.

programme.title: Jane Austen's Emma
programme.desc: A beautiful girl is happy to play matchmaker but fails to notice that her own heart has been captured.

programme.title: Operator
programme.desc: Chaos ignites when the daughter of a veteran 911 operator and her estranged husband are held captive.

programme.title: Transsiberian
programme.desc: A Transsiberian train journey to Moscow becomes a thrilling chase of deception and murder.

programme.title: Rough Air: Danger on Flight 534
programme.desc: A reluctant co-pilot is forced to take command of a passenger plane after the captain is severely injured.

programme.title: Gang of Roses
programme.desc: In this action-packed Western, the former leader of a disbanded all-female gang calls her comrades back to the saddle to hunt down hidden treasure and avenge the death of her sister.

programme.title: Aurora Teagarden
programme.desc: Aurora, passionnée d'intrigues policières, enquête sur le meurtre d'une vieille amie, dont le succès sur Internet attisait la jalousie.

programme.title: Aurora Teagarden
programme.desc: Une bibliothécaire met à contribution les membres de son club d'analyse de meurtres non élucidés pour enquêter sur un empoisonnement.

programme.title: Une rose pour sa tombe : l'histoire vraie de Cynthia Baumgartner
programme.desc: Lori est bouleversée lorsque sa meilleure amie se noie dans des circonstances mystérieuses lors d'une sortie en bateau avec son mari Randy.

programme.title: Cauchemar sur le campus
programme.desc: Une étudiante découvre les secrets mortels d'une sororité, en se lançant dans une quête pour retrouver et sauver sa meilleure amie disparue.

programme.title: The Admirable Crichton
programme.desc: The story of an English butler who, shipwrecked with his employers, leads them in an attempt to survive.

programme.title: What A Woman
programme.desc: A powerful agent's search for a movie's leading man sends her to a handsome, but dull, author.

programme.title: Harriet Craig
programme.desc: Harriet Craig runs a household, manipulating others in order to keep her place perfect, no matter the cost.

programme.title: Under The Yum Yum Tree
programme.desc: A couple in love agree to determine their "character compatability" by living together platonically.

programme.title: 12 Days of Christmas
programme.desc: During their first winter break from college, two childhood friends navigate turbulent reunions, unspoken romances, and even an unplanned pregnancy.

programme.title: Hit By Lightning
programme.desc: The loneliest man in North America finally meets the perfect woman but there's only one catch - she's married and wants him to kill her husband.

programme.title: London Mitchell's Christmas
programme.desc: London becomes faced with a diagnosis of prostate cancer which forces him to rethink his total outlook on life during the Christmas holiday.

programme.title: Santa Girl
programme.desc: Santa Claus� only daughter wants to experience the �real world� before she must marry the son of Jack Frost and take over the family business. Cassie Claus sets off to college while trying to keep her famous father a secret.

programme.title: Picture Locked: Matt Ogens
programme.desc: Filmmaker Matt Ogens breaks down his documentary "Confessions of a Superhero" which chronicles the lives of costumed performers on the Hollywood Walk of Fame. Directed by Yasir Masood & James Rohan.

programme.title: Still/Life
programme.desc: A cultural renaissance in the historic city of Yangon, and a rare glimpse into a country in extreme transition. Directed by Josh Hayward.

programme.title: The Blessings Of Aging
programme.desc: The sweet and sassy residents of a retirement community explain the many upsides of getting older. Directed by Jenny Schweitzer Bell.

programme.title: The Magic Of Chess
programme.desc: Filmed at the 2019 Elementary Chess Championships at the Nashville Opryland resort, a group of children share their insights about the benefits of chess. Directed by Jenny Schweitzer Bell.

programme.title: The Sentence of Michael Thompson
programme.desc: In 1994, Michael Thompson was arrested for selling three pounds of cannabis and was sentenced to 42 to 60 years in prison. He is the longest serving non-violent offender in the history of Michigan and he is finally up for clemency. Directed by Kyle Thrash and Haley Elizabeth Anderson. A DOCUMENTARY+ Original.

programme.title: The Starr Sisters
programme.desc: Patte and Randa Starr are fun specialists. After growing up in an abusive household set above their father's candy store, they spent their lives fighting to find joy and freedom. Now in their 70s, these sisters do exactly as they please: they live togther near the beach, they always have a movie on, and the candy drawer is fully stocked. Directed by Beth Einhorn and Bridey Elliott.

programme.title: The Thousand Year Journey
programme.desc: "Routine is the enemy of time." Jedidiah Jenkins rides his bicycle from Oregon to Patagonia, the southern tip of South America, while expounding upon ideas that made him leave a job he loved for the open road. Directed by Kenny Laubbacher.

programme.title: Tracing Light
programme.desc: A dive into the subconscious minds of Brooklyn based installation art duo, Hovver. Katherine Brice and Chris Lunney take natural light formations and isolate them in their art - this is their process. Directed by Alex Ramsey.

programme.title: What The Psychic Saw
programme.desc: Before filmmaker Matthew Palmer's birth, his mother visited a psychic. This is a film about that visit and the tragic - and fateful - prediction the psychic made on that day. Directed by Matthew Palmer.

programme.title: When The LAPD Blows Up Your Neighborhood
programme.desc: Tensions rise on a busy street as news organizations and local residents witness an extremely volatile situation turn into a literal powder keg. Directed by Nathan Truesdell. A DOCUMENTARY+ Original film.

programme.title: Wildfire
programme.desc: For Nicola, and other winemakers like him, this environmental change has existential consequences it threatens his livelihood. “Wildfire” patiently observes Nicola in this affective state of anxiety—as the wildfire grows within him—as he's forced to alter the course of his harvest in real time. Directed by Andree Ljutica.Nicola is a natural winemaker living in a remote village near Crotone, where his atavistic pursuit of wine is under active siege from climate related wildfires and the prolonged dry seasons.

programme.title: Artificial Intelligence: A Fork In The Road
programme.desc: We are at a fork in the road. Which path we take will dictate the course of the lives and livelihoods of billions of people. AI will either empower greater human agency…or it will undermine it. Directed by CJ Schmidt.

programme.title: Quiet Minds Silent Streets
programme.desc: Quiet Minds Silent Streets introduces viewers to a community that has been chronically affected by gun violence. This thoughtful, eye-opening documentary asks whether they can find a new way forward with a relatively simple technique: mindfulness and meditation. Executive produced by Director X and directed by Karen Chapman.

programme.title: After Skid Row
programme.desc: After Skid Row documents the journey of Barbie Carter as she navigates the transition to housed life following the brutal reality of a decade on the streets. Her unique story and compelling character help to both demystify and deeply personalize a homelessness epidemic that continues to swell across Los Angeles. Directed by Lindsey Hagen.

programme.title: Fight Or Flight
programme.desc: Fight or Flight chronicles the journey of the first female pilot employed by Colorado Parks and Wildlife. Drawn to flying as a means to flee her early trauma, she pursues a career in professional aviation and discovers she must first assemble the tools required to face her past and heal. Directed by Lindsey Hagen.

programme.title: Groundwork
programme.desc: Thailand is the world's largest exporter of rubber, and Chorthip is a keyfarmer demonstrating and leading a shift to regenerative agroforestry methods. She's transformed her land from a rubber monoculture to a diverse rubber forest garden, implementing organic practices that improve the quality of life for both her family and hercommunity.

programme.title: Groundwork
programme.sub-title: A Family Journey Into Regenerative Cotton
programme.desc: When a family cotton farm defies the conventions holding them back—they embark on a journey of transformation that radically shifts their farm, their family, and their future. Directed by Lindsey Hagen.

programme.title: Creating Things
programme.desc: Two brothers use interview fragments, pieces of art, and various family mementos to explore the legacy of their father and what he left behind. Directed by Bryan and Taylor Simpson.

programme.title: Dancer Boy
programme.desc: When Lou Wilson makes fun of Parker Seaman's dancing, Parker (a grown man) signs up to compete in a children's dance contest to prove to Lou, everyone and even to himself, that he's a great dancer. Directed by Parker Seaman.

programme.title: NF Forward
programme.desc: Identical twins, Adam and Neil Pearson, are navigating life in South London with a rare genetic disorder, neurofibromatosis. Despite their identical DNA, the twins are affected in vastly different ways. The one thing that remains the same is their perspective of life and how to live it best. Directed by Jonathan Braue.

programme.title: NF Forward
programme.desc: Then a fateful moment for a family in North Carolina would change his life forever. Directed by Jonathan Braue.Maddox Gibson is a fun-loving 5th grader with a big smile who loves Legos and wants to be a chef. But in 2017, Maddox was a 10 year-old in China who had been living in an orphanage his entire life. Complicating his life even more was a tumor on his optic nerve caused by NF that had grown out of control, resulting in loss of sight and significant disfigurement of his left eye.

programme.title: NF Forward
programme.desc: Kylie suffers from a rare genetic disease called Neurofibromatosis which from birth affected her left eye. She has never been able to see from it, as she got older the plexiform tumors caused her eye to be removed. Against odds Kylie, a bubbly 16yr old is a high performing athlete, a back spotter in the Texas Elite Cheer team. While facing crippling mental blocks and battling low muscle tone because of her condition she preserves in a sport that favors not only physical aesthetics but requires precision, strength and flawless technique.

programme.title: NF Forward
programme.desc: Travis has lived in the fear of breaking his bones most of his life. From the age of 2 a tumor, caused by a rare disease called Neurofibromatosis, has been growing from his spine down his leg. Directed by Rory K. Mcharg.

programme.title: NF Forward
programme.desc: Aidan Fraser has undergone 20 major surgeries and been on five different chemotherapies in 17 years due to NF. Yet, despite the magnitude of this challenges, Aidan is a video blogger, YouTube channel host, CTF staff member, and fervent NF trailblazer with an incredible story of exceptional resilience. Directed by Jamoody Jaafar.

programme.title: Keith Jarrett
programme.desc: Lugano Estival Jazz is an annual open-air music festival that takes place over five days at the picturesque city of Lugano, Switzerland. Since 1979, this musical Mecca has been a must-attend event for all jazz and world music fans worldwide. On July 3, 1986, American pianist and composer Keith Jarrett is joined by bassist Gary Peacock and drummer Jack DeJohnette at Estival Jazz Lugano. Known as the Standards Trio, they put on an enthralling performance that shows these master musicians at their best.

programme.title: Christian McBride
programme.sub-title: Josh Evans - Ballad Of Ernie Washington
programme.desc: On July 20, 2018, double bassist Christian McBride presented his band ‘New Jawn' at the Malta Jazz Festival. A five-time Grammy winner, McBride is one of the most requested, most recorded, and most respected figures in the music world today. Hailing from Philadelphia, this music luminary combines jazz, R&B, pop/rock, hip hop/neo-soul, and classical. Gracing the Malta Jazz stage with him are Nasheet Waits (drums), Marcus Strickland (tenor sax), and Josh Evans (trumpet).

programme.title: Manu Dibango, Daniel Rallo, Jerry Malekani & Frederic Gaillardet
programme.sub-title: Soul Makossa: Manu Dibango in Stuttgart
programme.desc: Manu Dibango, one of Africa's biggest musical stars and top saxophonists, is an indispensable artist. He was one of the first African musicians to score a worldwide hit with his 1972 release “Soul Makossa”, paving the way for many other artists. The very concept of world music owes much to him. A superb stage performer with great communicative humour, Dibango is above all one of the founding fathers of a modern African musical style that is open to jazz, R&B, salsa, gospel, funk, and reggae. This concert showcases all these genres, and features an excellent line-up of musicians from the four corners of the planet.

programme.title: Stochelo Rosenberg & Peter Beets
programme.desc: The treasured compositions of guitarist Django Reinhardt, the founding father of hot club jazz, are engraved in everyone's memory. Although Reinhardt missed two fingers of his left hand, his virtuoso technique was unrivalled. Dutch guitarist Stochelo Rosenberg and pianist Peter Beets now have the pleasure to bring Django's music back to life with their infectious improvisations.

programme.title: Wes Montgomery
programme.sub-title: Herbie Man & Sonny Sharrock - Memphis underground
programme.desc: This selection of unforgettable performances by some of the most iconic and influential jazz guitarists in history showcases these legends' unique essence. Revel in Wes Montgomery's virtuosity as he performs the captivating “Nica's Dream” in Holland in 1965, and delight in the expressive beauty of his rendition of “Here's That Rainy Day”, recorded in London in that same year. Experience the timeless appeal of Joe Pass's “You Stepped Out of a Dream” and “You Turned The Tables on Me”, recorded live in Hanover in 1975. The interplay between Jim Hall and Atilla Zoller borders on the impossible as they perform “Extensions” in Hanover in 1973. Enjoy Jim Hall's lyrical solo performance of “I'm Getting Sentimental Over You” from 1964. Witness Sonny Sharrock's avant-garde fusion of jazz and rock in an exhilarating performance of “Peanut", recorded live in France in 1970. Sharrock is joined by flutist Herbie Mann in a soulful performance of “Memphis Underground” from 1969. Immerse yourself in the magic of jazz guitar!

programme.title: B.B.King
programme.sub-title: B.B. King Orchestra - The North Sea Jazz
programme.desc: The North Sea Jazz Festival is the largest indoor music festival in the world, known globally as the event where the past, present and future of jazz are featured within three days. Next to a firm base of jazz as the festival's staple music genre, many others, such as blues, soul, funk, or hip hop, pass by. Back in 1979, legendary American blues guitarist B.B. King gave an unforgettable performance for the audience in The Hague. Born in 1925 in a small town in Mississippi, 'Lucille' (as he nicknamed his guitar) helped him escape a miserable existence picking cotton. He arrived in Memphis in the late 1940s, where his cousin Bukka White taught him the tricks of the blues. Soon he developed his own style. King's virtuoso, narrative solos with lots of vibrato, have been an inspiration for most (rock) guitarists after him.

programme.title: Bill Evans, Chuck Israels & Larry Bunker
programme.sub-title: Bill Evans' European Tour: Live in '64, '65 & '70
programme.desc: Bill Evans: Live in '64, '65 & '70 features five separate performances by one of the most revered pianists in music history. This collection covers an 11-year span, highlighting Evans' ever-deepening artistry as he performs with four different all-star rhythm sections, including the stunning duo of bassist Niels-Henning Ørsted Pedersen and drummer Alan Dawson. This recording includes Evans' renditions of the rarely performed “Sareen Jurer,” “Blue Serge”, and “Twelve Tone Tune Two.” Among the many surprises in this collection is a live performance of “My Melancholy Baby” with saxophonist Lee Konitz.

programme.title: Herbert Blomstedt & Gewandhausorchester Leipzig
programme.sub-title: Symphonies in D by Mozart and Voríšek
programme.desc: Conductor Herbert Blomstedt and the Gewandhausorchester take us on a musical journey to the Czech Republic in this 2020 concert from Leipzig's Gewandhaus. The program opens with Bohemian composer Jan Václav Hugo Voríšek's Symphony in D major, Op. 23 (1821). Although the composition – Voríšek's only symphony – was never performed during the composer's lifetime, it has become one of his most-performed works today. Also on the program is Wolfgang Amadeus Mozart's Symphony No. 38 in D major, K. 504. This work, which premiered while Mozart was visiting Prague in 1787, is often referred to as the ‘Prague Symphony'.

programme.title: amarcordplus & Sebastian Heindl
programme.sub-title: Baroque choral music by Bach, Schütz et al.
programme.desc: Five-strong vocal ensemble amarcord is joined by an extra alto and two soprano voices for this unusual concert at Leipzig's St. Thomas Church. As amarcordplus, the ensemble presents a program focused on the city of Leipzig, which includes sacred madrigals by Johann Hermann Schein, motets from Heinrich Schütz's collection Geistliche Chor-Music, as well as two motets by the most famous Thomaskantor: Johann Sebastian Bach. Moreover, various sacred works by lesser-known contemporaries Philipp Heinrich Erlebach and mononymous composer Liebhold are performed, in addition to compositions by other members of the Bach family, including Johann Michael Bach and Johann Christoph Bach. This performance was recorded on May 1, 2020.

programme.title: Les Dissonances
programme.desc: Cinematic classical clips and energizing concert pieces featuring up-and-coming artists.

programme.title: Soloists of the Lucerne Festival Orchestra, Lucas Macías Navarro, Stefan Dohr & Jonathan Wegloop
programme.desc: The soloists from the Lucerne Festival Orchestra present two septets in this engaging 2020 concert, recorded at Lucerne Culture and Congress Center (KKL), Switzerland. It opens with Wolfgang Amadeus Mozart's Divertimento No. 11 in D major, K. 251, known as the ‘Nannerl-Septet'. Mozart composed it in 1776, probably on the occasion of his sister Maria Anna's name day, whose nickname was ‘Nannerl'. The septet is scored for oboe, two horns, two violins, viola and double bass. The program continues with Ludwig van Beethoven's Septet in E-flat major, Op. 20, which is scored for clarinet, horn, bassoon, violin, viola, cello and double bass. This 1799 piece achieved great popularity during Beethoven's lifetime. It was arranged for many various instrumental ensembles, including versions for two guitars, and piano four-hands, but also a trio for clarinet (or violin), cello, and piano, arranged by Beethoven himself.

programme.title: Gewandhausorchester Leipzig
programme.desc: Cinematic classical clips and energizing concert pieces featuring up-and-coming artists.

programme.title: Leonardo García Alarcón, Cappella Mediterranea & Mariana Flores
programme.sub-title: Vocal baroque works by Cavalli, Strozzi & Bembo
programme.desc: Argentinian conductor and harpsichordist Leonardo García Alarcón leads his ensemble Cappella Mediterranea in a program of Italian Baroque music. The ensemble performs some of the finest Baroque pieces composed by Francesco Cavalli, a prominent composer in 17th-century Venice, and two of his famous students, Barbara Strozzi and Antonia Bembo. Argentinian soprano Mariana Flores presents the vocal works. On the program are ‘Mira questi due lumi' from Cavalli's Le nozze di Teti e di Peleo, ‘Dimmi, Amor, che farò' from L'Oristeo, ‘Sinfonia della notte' from L'Egisto, and ‘E vuol dunque Ciprigna' from Ercole Amante ‘M'ingannasti in verità' from Bembo's Produzioni armoniche consacrate a Luigi XIV Strozzi's Sino alla morte, Che si può fare, Lagrime mie, L'amante segreto, and è Pazzo il moi core Biagio Marini's La Romanesca Tarquinio Merula's Aria Sopra La Cieccona and Dario Castello's Sonata Seconda. This performance was recorded at the magnificent Église Notre-Dame of Sablé-sur-Sarthe, France, on August 26, 2020.

programme.title: Neeme Järvi
programme.desc: Cinematic classical clips and energizing concert pieces featuring up-and-coming artists.

programme.title: Les Arts Florissants & Paul Agnew
programme.desc: Italian composer Claudio Monteverdi (1567-1643) composed nine books of madrigals in half a century, which helped transform the genre from the polyphonic, a cappella madrigals of the late Renaissance to the ‘concertato' madrigals of the early Baroque, shifting the style's focus to the ability of music to express emotions contained in a text. In 2011, British tenor Paul Agnew and renowned Baroque ensemble Les Arts Florissants started recording eight books of Monteverdi's madrigals. In this program, Agnew leads Les Arts Florissants in the madrigals of Book I. Published on New Year's day of 1587, Book I consist of seventeen madrigals for five voices. This performance was recorded at the Cité de la musique in Paris, France, in 2011.

programme.title: Protest Songs
programme.desc: Protest songs were a union between the emerging Folk music of the late 50s and early 60s, and the Civil Rights movement. They needed songs, which could be sung on marches, at causes, and could be anthems for the Civil Rights cause. And when Civil Rights wasn't so much achieved, but when it merged into the anti-war protest movement, then those Protest songs were also the soundtrack of that. The Vietnam War was seen as something, which was highly questionable. The new generation was thinking about that. So it was very much defining themselves against what had come before, against what was seen as an oppressive, hierarchical, patriarchal system, which had worked essentially during the war years, but didn't work anymore in the post-war years.

programme.title: Songs for the People
programme.desc: Protest songs about the conditions experienced in the 1920s and 1930s were written using traditional folk hillbilly/bluegrass traditions. Acts such as The Freedom Singers overthrowing 50s attitudes, bringing issues such as the Civil Rights Movement of the late 1950s and early 1960s into focus. This gave folk music a tremendous strength in the US creating such legends as Joan Baez, Woody Guthrie and Pete Seeger.

programme.title: Various Artists
programme.desc: The best of Qello Concerts

programme.title: Eric Clapton & Steve Winwood
programme.desc: History rocked and rock made history during a sold-out three-gig stand at Madison Square Garden in late February 2008 when icons Eric Clapton and Steve Winwood performed a concert together for the first time since their all-too-brief days in the legendary Blind Faith in 1969. Whether they will ever team again is unknown. But with Eric Clapton and Steve Winwood Live From Madison Square Garden fans everywhere are able to experience the sound and the glory of a performance on that famous stage by two of rock's greatest artists.

programme.title: M.I.A. & D.E.A

programme.title: Four Wheel Surprise
programme.desc: First, Lou and Matt confront a big-time player and his bodyguards at a Brazilian restaurant. Then, Sonia and Lyndah head to a yard sale to get a vehicle away from the car's owner and her out-of-control friend. And finally, the whole team hits the beach in an effort to get a truck-load of ATV's away from a gang of knife-wielding beach bums.

programme.title: Airplane Arrest
programme.desc: Lou and Froy repo an airplane, but Froy finds himself in handcuffs when Lou takes off. Matt joins Sonia on a repo from a pole dancing studio, but gets more than he bargained for.

programme.title: Matt & Froy Auto Recovery
programme.desc: First, Lou and Sonia make their way to a home in the hills to clean a germaphobe of his car. Then, it's Matt and Froy's big repo. Having left the company to start their own, they head out after a high-end exotic car from outside a mansion. And finally, Lou, Lyndah, and Lou's cousin Rob drive out to a used car lot to pick up a few cars, but the owner has been hurt by the economy and now he's ready to do the hurting.

programme.title: Sugardaddy Shakedown
programme.desc: The team wonders what's up with Sonia as she wrestles with Lou's big secret. Ronnie goes undercover and takes a gold digging RO out on a date, but when Carlos lets the cat out of the bag all hell breaks loose. Matt and Froy struggle with language barriers when an Italian photographer and his assistant resorts to violence because they can't comprehend. Will Sonia finally reveal what's happening, or will carrying her brother's burden get the best of her?

programme.title: It Ain't No Joke!
programme.desc: In this kick-off to the final season, the cast takes viewers back through the most outrageous, comical, and memorable repos of the series. And, get a sneak peek of the final season of Operation Repo.

programme.title: Hooligans and Explosions
programme.desc: Will a frying pan-wielding housewife stop Lou and Sonia from getting their car? Plus, a repo involving a truck filled with black market contraband goes horrifyingly wrong for Matt and Froy.

programme.title: Natural Liar
programme.desc: First, Froy and Ronnie dodge tomatoes and pesticide when an organic farmer won't give up his tractor, and when they are in the clear, Froy confronts Ronnie about telling Sonia about the boob purchase and inadvertently reveals the Lambo repo. Next, Carlos and Matt get an offer of stardom from a slick Hollywood agent. And finally, Sonia drags more information from Ronnie and doesn't understand why everyone's going behind her back with Froy's big Lambo repo. Plus, they lose control of a Chevy Impala while repoing from a mace wielding shopper.

programme.title: The Junior Partner
programme.desc: Now that Lou has accepted Matt's offer to buy into the company, the team is back in action, but there are still some unanswered questions. Sonia, Lyndah, and Froy interrupt a rich kid's brunch party to take away his ride, Matt and Lyndah attempt to repo an unusual vehicle from a man who thinks he's Uncle Sam, and Lou and Matt are in a losing match with an unruly gang of jacked-up rugby players.

programme.title: The Final Finale
programme.desc: On this special one hour series finale of Operation Repo, the team are scrambling to get ready for the huge Yacht repo to secure the sale of the company and the distribution of their severance packages, while still going after every day repos. In this episode, Sonia and Froy find a payment procrastinating pervert in the park, Matt and Ronnie put the brakes on a rich racecar driver, Matt and Carlos get stuck in the middle of sign maker's gluing process, and Ronnie and Carlos try to reason with an irresponsible retro racist. And finally, on their most dangerous repo ...

programme.title: Broken Bones and Broken Phones
programme.desc: First, Froy, Lou, and Matt wrestle a group of body builders when going after a Jeep Commander. Next, an unemployed stock broker gets a rude awakening when Lou and Lyndah wake him up inside his car. And finally, Froy learns why you should never text and not drive to pick up Sonia, because she breaks his phone in two.

programme.title: Missed Dates
programme.desc: First, Lyndah is put in a tough situation - she has to control a sobbing, cop-calling debtor and turn away Frankie's unwanted and inappropriate advances - all at the same time. Then, Matt and Froy have two problems on their hands, when they decide to haul away both a stripped-down husk of a car and the phone number of its owner's coquettish and bathrobe-wearing ex-girlfriend. Finally, Lou, Froy, and Lyndah head way out into the country to wrangle with a case of mistaken identity and a trio of shotgun-wielding cultists.

programme.title: The Good Life
programme.desc: What does it take to have a good life? Science and advice on how to live well, find meaning and be happy. 1) Why having fun is the secret to a healthier life | Catherine Price 2) The philosophy of cynicism - William D. Desmond 3) How cohousing can make us happier (and live longer) | Grace Kim 4) Why do our bodies age? | Monica Menesini 5) What really matters at the end of life | BJ Miller

programme.title: How The Brain Works
programme.desc: How does the brain -- a 3-pound snarl of nervous tissue -- create inspired inventions, the feeling of hunger, the experience of beauty, the sense of self? Researchers at the edge of science explain. 1) How to hack your brain when you're in pain | Amy Baxter 2) How does your brain respond to pain? - Karen D. Davis 3) What causes seizures, and how can we treat them? | Christopher E. Gaw 4) The truth about unwanted arousal | Emily Nagoski 5) Hacking your memory -- with sleep | Matt Walker 6) Can you be awake and asleep at the same time? | Masako Tamaki

programme.title: What in the World?
programme.desc: Perspective-shifting ideas on how to see life on Earth. 1) How tsunamis work | Alex Gendler 2) Whoever builds something here will be rich beyond measure | Fabio Pacucci 3) How to grow a glacier | M Jackson 4) How light technology is changing medicine | Sajan Saini 5) How do ocean currents work? | Jennifer Verduin 6) What makes volcanoes erupt? | Steven Anderson 7) If superpowers were real: Immortality | Joy Lin 8) The science of symmetry | Colm Kelleher 9) Why is this 2,500 year old shipwreck so well-preserved? | Helen Farr and Jon Adams 10) Dark matter: The matter we can't see | James Gillies 11) The sonic boom problem | Katerina Kaouri

programme.title: Art and Science of Love
programme.desc: A crash course on what it means to love -- and love well. 1) A better way to talk about love | Mandy Len Catron 2) The Chinese legend of the butterfly lovers | Lijun Zhang 3) The mathematics of love | Hannah Fry 4) Why should you read "The God of Small Things" by Arundhati Roy? | Laura Wright 5) How understanding divorce can help your marriage | Jeannie Suk Gersen

programme.title: Secrets of the Wild
programme.desc: Be inspired to go out and rediscover the wonders of the natural world -- and get a deeper understanding of where (and why) the wild things grow. 1) Let's protect the oceans like national parks | David Lang 2) The real reason dodo birds went extinct | Leon Claessens 3) Stunning photos of the endangered Everglades | Mac Stone 4) The beauty of wildlife — and an artistic call to protect it | Isabella Kirkland 5) How community-led conservation can save wildlife | Moreangels Mbizah

programme.title: Tales from the Edge
programme.desc: Ski to the North Pole. Row across the Pacific. Swim on Mt. Everest. Adventurers and brave souls who have pushed themselves to the limits of human endurance, and share what they've learned. 1) To the South Pole and back — the hardest 105 days of my life | Ben Saunders 2) The dangerous race for the South Pole - Elizabeth Leane 3) The beautiful balance between courage and fear | Cara E. Yar Khan 4) What I learned from spending 31 days underwater | Fabien Cousteau 5) I leapt from the stratosphere. Here's how I did it | Alan Eustace

programme.title: The Latest in Ideas
programme.desc: New, fresh ideas straight from the brightest minds of our time. 1) Yes, you can be an entrepreneur too | Saamra Mekuria-Grillo 2) Can you transplant a head to another body? | Max G. Levy 3) Meet our planet's hidden defenders | Anjan Sundaram 4) The tragedy of the one guy who was right about the Trojan Horse | Noah Charney 5) The tipping points of climate change — and where we stand | Johan Rockström
